35. It is, therefore, clear that the accused is not liable for the offence 
of rape if the victim has wilfully agreed to maintain sexual relations. 
The Court has also recognised that a prosecutrix can agree to 
have sexual intercourse on account of her love and passion for the 
accused. 
36. This Court in Shivashankar v. State of Karnataka,9 had quashed 
criminal proceedings on the ground that it is difficult to hold sexual 
intercourse in the course of a relationship, which continued for eight 
years, as ‘rape’ especially when the complainant therein had alleged
